title: Add pre-push hook that re-runs the verification gate
description:
Close the gate-bypass gap (Goodhart / P1.gate-bypass).

**Problem:** `scripts/ralph/ralph.sh` (lines 313-319) parses `<gate-result>PASS</gate-result>` from the agent's own output. `GATE_RESULT` is logged but not wired into `AUTO_LAND`, so the agent can emit a PASS tag without running the gate. `project-kickoff-prompt.md` line 197 acknowledges the hole and even names the fix but does not ship it.

**Fix:** Add a `.git/hooks/pre-push` hook (installed by `scripts/hooks/install.sh`) that:
1. Extracts the verification gate command from `CLAUDE.md` (convention: fenced code block under `## Verification Gate`).
2. Runs it via `bash -c` and captures the real exit code.
3. Fails the push if the observed result diverges from the self-reported `<gate-result>` tag.

Reference sketch in `initializer-recommendations.md` section 1 (lines 21-50).

**Follow-ups in same bead:**
- Update decision register's "Verification truth" row: `ritual-bounded` → `bounded`.
- Remove the prose caveat at `project-kickoff-prompt.md` line 197 (instance of the repo's own "promote, don't repeat" rule).
- Document the split in `CLAUDE.md`.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-push hook extracts gate command from CLAUDE.md, re-runs it, fails if exit code diverges from agent's self-reported <gate-result> tag. Installer block added to scripts/hooks/install.sh. Decision register's 'Verification truth' row promoted from ritual-bounded to bounded. Prose caveat at project-kickoff-prompt.md line 197 removed once hook lands.

title: Final review: verify all initializer-audit changes against recommendations
description:
Gate bead for the initializer-audit set. Runs after impl beads 1–4 close.

**Procedure:**
1. Re-read `initializer-recommendations.md` start-to-finish.
2. For each of sections 1–4, verify the shipped code against the recommendation:
   - Does the mechanism exist where the recommendation said it should?
   - Does it do what the recommendation described (not just compile)?
   - Are the downstream promotions applied (decision-register row changes, prose-caveat deletion)?
3. Run `scripts/hooks/install.sh` against a clean worktree and confirm hooks install without error.
4. Run the new `tests/hooks/` suite and confirm green.
5. Simulate a gate-bypass attempt: emit `<gate-result>PASS</gate-result>` with a gate command that exits non-zero, and confirm the pre-push hook blocks it.
6. Simulate an unedited-rubric commit (once a bead exists) and confirm the pre-commit hook blocks it.

**If any check fails:** create a new impl bead that specifies the missing work, mark it `blocks` this review bead, and leave this bead open. Do not close until every downstream bead is closed and re-verified.

**Explicitly out of scope:** adding new audit findings. This bead verifies the four recommendations on file, not the whole codebase.
acceptance_criteria: Every fix in initializer-recommendations.md verified against the shipped implementation: (1) pre-push hook re-runs gate and blocks divergent self-reports; 'Verification truth' is now 'bounded'; prose caveat removed. (2) tests/hooks/ bats suite runs green and covers the listed edge cases; CONTRIBUTING.md orders parser tests before end-to-end. (3) rubric-edit hook blocks unedited starter; 'Review verdict' now 'bounded'. (4) CONTRIBUTING.md cleanup block carries inline comments. If any fix falls short, open new impl beads (blocking this one) that specify the missing work; do not close this review bead until all downstream beads are closed and verified.

title: Tighten rubric-edit guard: check for project-specificity, not just disclaimer phrase
description:
Discovered by review agent-template-6ij as P2.weak-test.

The rubric-edit guard in scripts/hooks/install.sh:163 fires only when 'This file is a starter rubric' literal phrase matches via grep -qF. A future edit that simply deletes that one sentence — without renaming the header, without adding any project-specific clause — passes the guard. The hook's BLOCKED message accurately describes the Phase 1 contract (replace the disclaimer + add at least one project-specific clause) but only the disclaimer half is enforced.

Suggested fix: in addition to the disclaimer check, assert (a) the rubric header line does not equal the shipped starter header verbatim AND (b) the rubric contains at least one clause that is not in a checked-in 'starter clauses' allowlist. The allowlist could live in scripts/hooks/install.sh as a named array and ship as the authoritative starter set.

Acceptance:
- Guard rejects a stub rubric with no clauses (currently passes).
- Guard rejects a rubric that copies the starter clauses verbatim with no additions.
- Guard accepts the current docs/skills/review-rubric.md (which has a project-named header and a project-specific P1.hook-bypass clause).
- New test in tests/hooks/ exercises all three cases.

Verified failure scenario reproduced in docs/reviews/agent-template-6ij.md.

title: Fix verification gate: trailing '|| true' masks failures in the whole chain
description:
The verification gate in CLAUDE.md reads:

  bash -n scripts/ralph/ralph.sh && bash -n scripts/hooks/install.sh && bash -n scripts/ralph/prompt.md 2>/dev/null || true

The trailing '|| true' was intended to suppress the expected failure of 'bash -n' on prompt.md (it's markdown, not bash). But due to shell precedence, '|| true' binds to the whole '&&' chain, so ANY failure in the chain (including real syntax errors in ralph.sh or install.sh) returns exit 0. The gate is currently a structural no-op.

Demonstrated:
  $ echo 'foo(' > /tmp/broken.sh
  $ bash -n /tmp/broken.sh && echo ok || true ; echo $?
  # → exit 0 despite parse failure

This also neutralizes the pre-push hook added by agent-template-4mw — the hook re-runs whatever the gate is, and if the gate always returns 0, the hook always passes.

Fix options:
  (a) Drop the prompt.md parse check entirely (markdown should never be parse-checked as bash).
  (b) Isolate the last clause: '... && { bash -n scripts/ralph/prompt.md 2>/dev/null || true; }'
  (c) Replace prompt.md check with a real markdown lint or drop it.

Acceptance: the gate in CLAUDE.md returns non-zero when any in-chain command fails.

title: Broaden gate.bats trailing-soft-fail check beyond '|| true'
description:
Discovered by review agent-template-6ij as P2.weak-test.

tests/hooks/gate.bats test 2 asserts the gate command does not end with the regex '[[:space:]]*\|\|[[:space:]]*true[[:space:]]*$'. The bug class documented in docs/failure-modes.md row 14 is broader: any soft-fail escape via shell precedence (|| true, || :, || 0, || exit 0, etc.) silently masks all earlier && clauses. Reproduced: replacing the gate with '... && bats tests/hooks/ || :' passes test 2 (the colon noop is functionally identical to || true but isn't matched).

The bug class is rescued by tests 6/7 (corruption tests detect that ralph.sh / parsers.sh syntax errors no longer fail the chain), but the named test 2 has a coverage gap that future agents reading it as the canonical defense will not notice.

Suggested fix: broaden test 2's regex to cover all bash builtins/operators that always-succeed: 'true', ':', '0', 'exit 0'. Or replace with a behavioral check: build a tiny temp gate '(false) <suffix>' and assert it exits non-zero.

Acceptance:
- gate.bats test 2 fails for all of: '|| true', '|| :', '|| 0', '|| exit 0' suffixes.
- gate.bats test 2 passes for the current gate.
- docs/failure-modes.md row 14 updated to point at the broadened check (or a renamed check).

Verified failure scenario reproduced in docs/reviews/agent-template-6ij.md.

title: Review artifact validator: enforce clause-name membership against docs/skills/review-rubric.md
description:
Discovered by review agent-template-6ij as P2.weak-test.

The review artifact validator in scripts/hooks/install.sh:397 requires at least one match of the regex 'P[123]\.[a-z][a-z-]*' in a review artifact. It does not check membership against the actual clauses defined in docs/skills/review-rubric.md. Reproduced: a review artifact citing 'P1.totally-made-up-clause' (no such clause in the rubric) passes the validator with exit 0.

The decision-register row 'Review verdict' (docs/decision-register.md:11) claims 'bounded' by 'each finding cites a clause' — but the binding is to clause-shaped strings, not to clauses that exist. This is a Goodhart on the validator itself.

Suggested fix: extract the canonical clause list from docs/skills/review-rubric.md (every '**P[123]\.<name>**' bullet under '## Severity definitions') and assert each cited clause in the review artifact is in that list. Reject any cited clause not in the rubric with a BLOCKED message naming the offending clause. New parser function in scripts/hooks/parsers.sh + new tests in tests/hooks/.

Acceptance:
- Validator rejects review artifact citing P1.invented-clause if no matching clause exists in the rubric.
- Validator accepts review artifact citing only clauses that exist in the rubric.
- Adding a new clause to docs/skills/review-rubric.md immediately makes that clause valid (no separate registration step).
- New tests/hooks/ tests for the membership check.

Verified failure scenario reproduced in docs/reviews/agent-template-6ij.md.

title: Add pre-commit hook that rejects unedited review-rubric starter
description:
Tightening (not a violation): `project-kickoff-prompt.md` line 105 states that the shipped starter alone does not satisfy the Phase 1 contract — an unedited starter means 'Review verdict' cannot legitimately be `bounded`. Every other Phase 1 contract that can be mechanically enforced is enforced; this is the only one where a one-line grep would promote `ritual-bounded` → `bounded` and reduce Phase-1-approval burden on the user.

**Fix:** Add the following block to the pre-commit hook generator in `scripts/hooks/install.sh`:

```bash
RUBRIC_FILE="$PROJECT_ROOT/docs/skills/review-rubric.md"
if [ -f "$RUBRIC_FILE" ]; then
  if grep -qF "This file is a starter rubric" "$RUBRIC_FILE"; then
    if [ -n "$IN_PROGRESS_BEAD" ]; then
      echo "BLOCKED: docs/skills/review-rubric.md still contains the starter disclaimer."
      # ... (full sketch in initializer-recommendations.md lines 140-157)
      exit 1
    fi
  fi
fi
```

Promote the "Review verdict" row in the decision register from `ritual-bounded` to `bounded` once this hook ships.

Reference sketch in `initializer-recommendations.md` section 3 (lines 127-159).
acceptance_criteria: Pre-commit hook block added to scripts/hooks/install.sh that rejects commits where docs/skills/review-rubric.md still contains the starter disclaimer once any bead exists (Phase 1 bootstrap exempted). 'Review verdict' row in decision register promoted from ritual-bounded to bounded once this hook ships.

title: Add automated parser tests for hook parsers (bats suite)
description:
Fill the Testing Pyramid gap: the awk parsers in the pre-commit hooks are the mechanism that every other register-integrity contract rests on, yet there are no automated tests.

**Problem:** `scripts/hooks/install.sh` (lines 256-525) and tag parsing in `scripts/ralph/ralph.sh` (lines 105-116, 205-222, 313-319) have real edge cases — escaped pipes, Unicode, exactly-5-column rows, trailing whitespace, multi-line continuation rows the hook is supposed to reject. `CONTRIBUTING.md` line 30 mandates an end-to-end ralph run but that does not exercise parser edge cases.

**Fix:**
1. Light refactor of `install.sh` to expose parser blocks as callable functions (currently inlined in the generated pre-commit script).
2. Add `tests/hooks/` with a bats or shunit2 suite. Cover at least:
   - fm register: rejects bad Status; accepts covered Status + existing check file.
   - decision register: rejects multi-line continuation rows.
   - CLAUDE.md model-tag validator: rejects unknown model tag; accepts valid tag.
   - Edge cases: escaped pipes, Unicode in cells, trailing whitespace.
3. Wire into `CONTRIBUTING.md` as the first verification step.

Reference sketch in `initializer-recommendations.md` section 2 (lines 68-123).
acceptance_criteria: tests/hooks/ directory contains bats (or shunit2) suite exercising failure-mode register integrity, decision register integrity, and CLAUDE.md model-tag validator parsers against known-good and known-bad fixtures (escaped pipes, Unicode cells, 5-column rows, trailing whitespace, multi-line continuations). scripts/hooks/install.sh refactored to expose parser blocks as callable functions so they can be tested outside of a live pre-commit context. CONTRIBUTING.md updated to run the parser suite as the first verification step before the end-to-end ralph run.

title: Annotate AGENTS.md reference in CONTRIBUTING.md cleanup block
description:
Cosmetic nit: `CONTRIBUTING.md` line 52 contains `rm -rf .beads AGENTS.md .pytest_cache`. `AGENTS.md` appears exactly once in the repo with no explanation — it is almost certainly a Sourcegraph Amp artifact but a new contributor would have to infer that.

**Fix:** Add explanatory comments after the cleanup command:

```markdown
rm -rf .beads AGENTS.md .pytest_cache
# .beads: created by `bd init`
# AGENTS.md: created by Amp on first run (analogous to Claude Code's CLAUDE.md)
# .pytest_cache: created if the test bead's verification gate runs pytest
```

Reference in `initializer-recommendations.md` section 4 (lines 163-176).
acceptance_criteria: CONTRIBUTING.md line 52 cleanup block includes inline comments identifying what creates each cleanup target (.beads from 'bd init', AGENTS.md from Amp on first run analogous to Claude Code's CLAUDE.md, .pytest_cache from pytest-based gate).

title: Grow verification gate to include parsers.sh syntax check and bats tests/hooks/
description:
Now that agent-template-8gi has removed the trailing '|| true' that silently masked every clause, the gate can safely grow. mhd's archive note explicitly deferred the bats append until 8gi shipped.

Append to the gate in CLAUDE.md:
  bash -n scripts/ralph/ralph.sh && bash -n scripts/hooks/install.sh && bash -n scripts/hooks/parsers.sh && bats tests/hooks/

Why parsers.sh: the pre-commit hook sources it at runtime (install.sh:77). A syntax error there breaks the whole register-integrity chain but is currently unguarded by the gate.

Why bats tests/hooks/: that's how parser regressions surface without chasing a loop failure (CONTRIBUTING.md already documents this as the manual step; bringing it into the gate makes it mechanical).

Update tests/hooks/gate.bats 'gate passes against the current repo' test to keep working under the expanded chain.
